title: MCP server TypeScript scaffold
description: package.json with @modelcontextprotocol/sdk. TypeScript project in leftglove/mcp-server/. Connects to SL sieve HTTP API at localhost:3333 (configurable). Reads glossary EDN files from SL project directory. One dummy tool to prove the pipe.

title: TL UI: Write Glossary integration
description: Write Glossary button in toolbar. Groups named elements by intent region, sends POST /glossary/intents to SL for each region. Shows confirmation (N elements across M regions written). Fetches GET /glossary to show already-graduated indicators on elements. Skips unnamed elements. Depends on SL glossary endpoints and Pass 2 naming mode.
notes: Absorbs leftglove-xs0 (glossary generation). SL endpoints already exist (leftglove-cms closed). This bead covers the full graduation flow: TL UI button → transform named elements to glossary format → POST /glossary/intents → confirmation display.

title: test-a
description: test

title: The Sieve — JS DOM inventory function
description: resources/sieve.js — deterministic JavaScript function for structured DOM inventory. sieve/core.clj — inject JS via bridge execute-script, parse results into Clojure data. sieve/taxonomy.clj — element classification (interactable/output/structural/image), region path derivation from landmarks and ARIA. Verify: from REPL, run sieve on a live page, get structured inventory matching the element record spec from ARCHITECTURE.md.
design: Files: resources/sieve.js (the JS function), leftglove/sieve/core.clj (orchestration — inject + parse), leftglove/sieve/taxonomy.clj (classification rules, region derivation). Consumes: bridge/browser execute-script. Produces: structured page inventory as Clojure data.
acceptance_criteria: From REPL: (sieve/run! browser) returns a map matching the page inventory spec from ARCHITECTURE.md. Elements have category, tag, label, locators, state, rect, region. Tested against at least two different sites.
notes: STALE: Sieve JS lives in SL and serves via HTTP. LG consumes it, doesn't build it. Rewrite this as sieve accuracy/regression testing task.
close_reason: Stale — sieve JS lives in SL, works via HTTP. Replaced by sieve regression test issue.

title: SVOI vocabulary projection as MCP tools
description: Read SL glossaries (subjects, verbs), interfaces, and intent regions via bridge. Project vocabulary as dynamic MCP tool schemas — agent sees available subjects, verbs per interface, objects per IR per interface. SVOI dispatch: compose step text from tuple components, execute via SL REPL functions (step/as). Auto-observe after every action. Action edge persistence to graph. Verify: agent sees SVOI vocabulary as tools, can execute an action, gets post-action observation, action edge in graph.
design: Files: leftglove/tools/svoi.clj (vocabulary reading, schema projection, dispatch), leftglove/mcp/schema.clj (SVOI-to-MCP-tool-schema transform). Consumes: bridge (glossaries, step registry, IRs), sieve (auto-observe), graph/writes (action edges). Produces: dynamic MCP tools from SVOI state.
acceptance_criteria: Agent sees dynamically generated tools reflecting loaded SVOI vocabulary. Agent executes a verb (e.g. click) on an object, gets post-action observation. Action edge exists in graph linking before/after observations.
notes: STALE: Rescope for demo. Demo needs: read glossary files → project as MCP tool schemas. Full SVOI dispatch + auto-observe + graph edges = post-demo.

title: Project skeleton and SL dependency
description: deps.edn with SL as local root dep, nREPL, test runner. Directory structure (src/leftglove/, test/leftglove/). Main entry point stub. Verify: clj -M:dev starts REPL with SL namespaces loadable.
design: Files: deps.edn, src/leftglove/main.clj. SL referenced as :local/root dep pointing to sibling directory. Test runner alias in deps.edn.
acceptance_criteria: clj -M:dev starts a REPL. (require 'shiftlefter.runner.config) succeeds. (require 'shiftlefter.graph) succeeds. clj -M:test runs and reports zero failures.
notes: STALE: Was Clojure skeleton. MCP server will be TypeScript. Rewrite as TS project scaffold with @modelcontextprotocol/sdk.
close_reason: Stale — was Clojure skeleton, MCP server is TypeScript. Replaced by new scaffold issue.

title: Progressive tool disclosure
description: Control which SVOI-derived tools are advertised based on session maturity. Cold start: only observe. As vocabulary loads, action tools appear.

title: IR resolution via sieve matching
description: Compute which intent regions are active on the current page by matching IR element bindings against sieve output. Coarse matching first (id, name, testid), hybrid browser verification for ambiguous cases. See ARCHITECTURE.md Open Design section.

title: test-b
description: test

title: Pull GitHub release into fresh directory
description: Clone ShiftLefter from GitHub (public release) into a new directory. This is the known-clean starting point for modernization.

title: Push modernized SL to GitHub org
description: Push the clean, modernized repo to ShiftLefter GitHub organization. Set up LG as second repo in the same org. Verify README, LICENSE, and docs are current.

title: Beads init and modern scaffolding for SL
description: beads init --prefix sl. Write CLAUDE.md (stack module, build commands, key abstractions). Write ARCHITECTURE.md (parser, step engine, browser protocol, SVOI, graph, intent system). Write CONTEXT.md (project constraints, roles, workflow). SL is now operational with modern practices.
notes: SL 0.4.0 exists as tag but no GitHub release. Previous tags (0.3.6) have built jars. Need release process as part of scaffolding — will recur as we build.
close_reason: Scaffolding complete: beads init, CLAUDE.md, CONTEXT.md done. ARCHITECTURE.md tracked separately as sl-i1z.

title: Convert SL roadmap and epics into labeled beads
description: Read existing roadmap from _docs/reconciled/shiftlefter-roadmap.md and any other planning docs. Convert work items into beads issues in the new SL repo. Label by milestone. Wire dependencies (once dep-add bug is fixed).
close_reason: Superseded — roadmap being rebuilt demo-backwards in current planning session

title: Diff GitLab HEAD against GitHub release, apply source changes
description: Diff the GitLab private repo (HEAD) against the GitHub public release. Identify source-only changes (Asami, Playwright, etc. — not _docs). Apply the source diff to the fresh copy to reach feature parity with clean history.
close_reason: GitLab patches applied, SL serving sieve at localhost:3333

title: observe tool with graph persistence
description: tools/observe.clj — wire sieve into MCP tool with resolution parameter. graph/schema.clj — specs for observation nodes and action edges. graph/writes.clj — persist observation to Asami via bridge. Register observe in MCP server tool registry. Verify: agent calls observe via MCP, gets structured inventory, observation node exists in Asami.
design: Files: leftglove/tools/observe.clj (MCP tool impl), leftglove/graph/schema.clj (node/edge specs), leftglove/graph/writes.clj (Asami persistence). Consumes: sieve/core, bridge graph functions, mcp/server tool registry. Produces: observe tool registered in MCP server, observations in graph.
acceptance_criteria: Agent calls observe via MCP, gets structured inventory in response. Observation node with inventory, URL, timestamp exists in Asami after call. query over observations returns the persisted node.
notes: STALE: Split needed. MCP observe tool (demo-critical) vs Asami graph persistence (post-demo). Observe is just a proxy to POST /sieve.
close_reason: Stale — split: observe tool absorbed into MCP server, graph persistence deferred.

title: query_graph and annotate tools
description: tools/graph.clj — query_graph MCP tool, accepts Datalog queries, runs against Asami via bridge, returns results. tools/annotate.clj — annotate MCP tool, writes semantic labels to graph as annotation nodes. Verify: agent can query observations from graph, agent can annotate an element, annotation persists.
design: Files: leftglove/tools/graph.clj (query tool), leftglove/tools/annotate.clj (annotate tool), leftglove/graph/query.clj (query helpers). Consumes: bridge graph functions, mcp/server tool registry. Produces: query_graph and annotate tools registered in MCP server.
acceptance_criteria: Agent calls query_graph with Datalog, gets results. Agent calls annotate with target + label, annotation node persists in graph. Annotation queryable via query_graph.

title: Triage _docs from old SL copy
description: Walk through _docs/ subdirectories (active, reconciled, canon, unreconciled, archive, discard, private, unsure). Pull valuable items into docs/ or notes/ in the new copy. Discard the rest. Requires Chair — only Gabriel knows what's valuable.

title: CI pipeline for SL
description: GitHub Actions: run tests on push (clj -M:test), lint (clj -M:kondo), compliance suite. Team members need fast feedback on PRs.

title: Bridge — SL config loading and browser provisioning
description: bridge/shiftlefter.clj — accept path to project under test, load SL config via config/load-config-safe, load step definitions. bridge/browser.clj — provision browser instance via SL adapter registry. All paths absolute (no SL_USER_CWD needed). Verify: from REPL, init bridge with a shiftlefter.edn path, open a browser, navigate to a URL.
design: Files: leftglove/bridge/shiftlefter.clj (init, config, steps), leftglove/bridge/browser.clj (provision, navigate, execute-script wrapper). Consumes: SL runner.config, stepengine.registry, adapters.registry. Produces: initialized bridge state that sieve and tools use.
acceptance_criteria: From REPL: (bridge/init! {:config-path "/abs/path/to/shiftlefter.edn"}) succeeds. (bridge/browser! :web) provisions a browser. (bridge/navigate! browser "https://example.com") loads the page. Step registry is populated after init.
notes: STALE: Bridge no longer provisions browsers — SL handles that behind HTTP. Bridge is now: read glossary EDN files + call SL HTTP endpoints. Much smaller scope.
close_reason: Stale — bridge is now HTTP calls to SL. Absorbed into MCP server task.

title: Toddler loop UI v0 — screenshot + SVG overlay + keyboard classification
description: Build the toddler loop UI as a single HTML/JS file at leftglove/toddler/index.html. No build step, no npm, no framework. Open in browser, works immediately. DATA SOURCE: Fetches from SL's sieve HTTP endpoint (localhost:3333). - POST /sieve → JSON inventory (elements with categories, labels, locators, rects, regions) - GET /screenshot → PNG of current page - POST /navigate → tell SL to go to a URL - GET /status → current browser state LAYOUT: Full-page screenshot with SVG overlays on top, fixed bottom panel (200-250px) with element info and controls. NOT a modal, NOT a second window. See leftglove/notes/toddler-loop.md § UI Layout for the full design. PASS 1 — CLASSIFY (breadth-first, rapid-fire): - Show screenshot with current element highlighted (cyan border) - Already-classified elements get colored borders (green=clickable, blue=typable, yellow=readable, gray=chrome, purple=custom) - Bottom panel left: element info (tag, type, sieve label, region, locators) - Bottom panel right: category buttons with keyboard shortcuts c=clickable, t=typable, r=readable, x=chrome, u=custom, /=split, .=skip - One keystroke advances to next element - Progress counter: #17/47 - Arrow keys to go back/forward PASS 2 — LABEL (not needed for v0, but leave room in the data model): - Will add name input field, accept/skip/merge buttons later CONTROLS: - 'Sieve' button → POST /sieve, GET /screenshot, re-render - 'Navigate' button or URL bar → POST /navigate with URL, then auto-sieve - Classification summary shown after all elements classified SVG OVERLAY: - Screenshot displayed as at full page dimensions, scrollable - SVG layer on top with same dimensions - Rects drawn from element.rect (absolute page coordinates) - Current element: thick cyan border, slight background tint - Classified elements: colored borders by category - Unclassified: thin dashed gray border - Chrome elements: very subtle, almost invisible border STATE: - Keep classification state in a JS object: {elementIndex: 0, classifications: {}, pass: 1} - Save/load state to localStorage so you don't lose progress on refresh - Export button: dump classifications as JSON IMPORTANT: The sieve returns elements with rects in absolute page coordinates. The SVG viewBox must match the full-page screenshot dimensions. The screenshot and SVG must scroll together.
close_reason: Toddler loop UI v0 built — single HTML file with screenshot+SVG overlay, keyboard classification (c/t/r/x/u), localStorage persistence, export

title: TL UI: load pre-labeled state
description: Load button or drag-drop that accepts classification JSON and restores full state. For demo: load pre-classified state so we skip classifying 47 elements on stage.

title: Sieve regression test suite
description: Known test pages with expected element inventories. Script sieves each page, compares against expected output. Start with 01-basics.html (13 elements). Catches regressions in detection, classification, labels, rects.

title: MCP protocol layer and dummy tool
description: JSON-RPC message parsing over stdio. MCP lifecycle (initialize/initialized handshake, capability negotiation). Tool registry and dispatch. One dummy echo tool to prove the pipe. Verify: an MCP client can connect, call the echo tool, get a response.
design: Files: leftglove/mcp/protocol.clj (JSON-RPC parsing/framing), leftglove/mcp/server.clj (tool registry, dispatch, capability negotiation), leftglove/main.clj (stdio loop). Consumes: nothing from SL. Produces: working MCP server skeleton that other tools register into.
acceptance_criteria: MCP client (e.g. Claude Desktop or mcp-cli) connects via stdio. initialize handshake completes with server capabilities. Client calls echo tool, gets response. Invalid JSON-RPC returns proper error codes.
notes: MERGE WITH leftglove-05g. With @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, the scaffold IS the protocol layer — the SDK handles JSON-RPC, lifecycle, capability negotiation. These are one task. Do 05g first (project setup), then 637 is just registering the echo tool and verifying handshake.

title: Raw SVOI execution path in SL
description: SL enhancement: a public function that takes an SVOI tuple and dispatches directly, without step text composition and regex matching. Eliminates the MVP hack of composing step text.

title: Autonomous toddler exploration — multi-page crawl with state isolation
description: The sieve is fast enough (~50ms for 409 elements on Slashdot) that letting toddlers wander between pages is achievable. Design: toddler follows links, sieves each page, builds cross-page inventory. Needs constraints to prevent parallel toddlers from corrupting each other's state — e.g., unauthenticated-only mode, separate browser contexts, or turn-based exploration. The sorting/classification UI also needs significant work to handle this volume (409 elements per page × N pages). Needs grooming: state isolation model, UI for multi-page inventory, crawl limits, duplicate detection across pages.

title: End-to-end demo rehearsal
description: All demo acts work in sequence. Practiced run-through. Identify timing issues and fallback options. Prepare pre-captured data for fallback. Integration, not new code.

title: SL: Auto-start sieve server from REPL
description: Add sieve server auto-start to dev/user.clj so it launches on REPL boot. Default: port 3333, headed Chrome. Controllable via env var (e.g. SIEVE=0 to skip) for sessions that don't need a browser. user.clj already does spec instrumentation on startup — add sieve-server/start! after that. Store the env atom in a def so it's accessible from the REPL (e.g. (def sieve-env ...)). Print the sieve server URL in the startup banner. SL CODEBASE WORK — modify dev/user.clj.
close_reason: Auto-start sieve server from dev/user.clj on REPL boot, SIEVE=0 to skip

title: Persistence — auto-save TL working state to files
description: Save toddler loop working state (classifications, names, intent regions, screenshots, observation history) to a known directory on disk. Currently localStorage-only — state dies with the browser tab, can't be shared, can't survive across machines. Naming convention for multiple sieve sessions (URL-based? timestamp? both?). Multiple people working on same app need to share state. The export JSON is close to the intermediate format already. For the demo, load-pre-labeled (leftglove-4j8) is sufficient. For the product, auto-save is table stakes. The intermediate format is working state / scratch paper — the glossary in git is the source of truth, but the working state needs to persist between sessions.

title: SL: Glossary HTTP endpoints on sieve server
description: Add two endpoints to SL's sieve HTTP server (server.clj). POST /glossary/intents — accepts JSON intent data, reads existing EDN file if present, merges new elements (new wins on collision, never removes), writes back. GET /glossary — returns current glossary state as JSON (all loaded subjects, verbs, intents). Merge semantics: (update existing :elements merge new-elements). If no intent file exists, create it. Paths derived from shiftlefter.edn :glossaries config. SL CODEBASE WORK — modify shiftlefter/sieve/server.clj and add glossary write helpers.
close_reason: Glossary convention root + HTTP endpoints on sieve server

title: TL UI: sieve diff display
description: Sieve twice, show what appeared/disappeared/changed. New elements get distinct highlight, removed shown as phantoms. For demo Act 3: add checkbox, re-sieve, diff shows +1 element.

title: MCP vocabulary projection — glossary to MCP tools
description: Read SL glossary files (subjects.edn, verbs/web.edn, glossary/intents/*.edn). Parse EDN. Project as dynamic MCP tool schemas. Agent sees Login.email, Login.password, Login.submit as references. Builds on MCP scaffold.

title: Observation loop — declared intent click
description: Human clicks element in SVG overlay → TL UI tells SL to execute that click on the live DOM (POST /click or similar SL endpoint) → wait for quiescence → re-sieve → element identity matching → diff display. This is the core exploration mechanism: 'Daddy tells the toddler to click #17.' The human directs, the toddler executes, the system records intent + outcome. Also supports programmatic exploration: 'what happens if you click #17?' After click: new observation, diff from previous, names carried forward. May need new SL endpoint for 'click element by locator' or can compose from existing sieve server capabilities.

title: Element identity across observations
description: When TL UI re-sieves (after code change, click, or navigation), match elements between old and new inventories to carry forward classifications and names. Matching tiers: (1) stable locators (id, testid, name) → automatic carry-forward, (2) composite key (region + tag + label) → high confidence, (3) unmatched → flag as new or removed. Persistent elements (nav, footer) carry names forward automatically. New elements enter the toddler loop. The 80/20 applies: composite key handles most cases, human reviews the rest. Without this, every re-sieve is a blank slate and diff display can only show counts, not 'Login.remember-me appeared.'

title: TL UI: timing display in status bar
description: Show sieve performance: Sieved 409 elements in 127ms. Measure round-trip for POST /sieve. Useful for demo and debugging.

title: TL UI: Pass 2 naming mode
description: Add Pass 2 mode to toddler loop UI. Mode switch after Pass 1 complete or manual toggle. Bottom panel right side gets: name input field with smart pre-fill (testid > id > name > label-transform), intent region field (auto-derived from sieve region, editable, can create new), locator display (show all available, highlight selected best). Arrow keys still navigate. Named elements show names on SVG overlay. Data model addition: names{} and intentRegions{} alongside classifications{}. Persists to localStorage.

title: Collection detection — repeated elements with shared structure
description: The sieve must detect DOM siblings that share a structural template — e.g., 30 story links on Hacker News, 50 Q&A pairs on a corporate FAQ, card grids on any aggregator (Slashdot, Reddit, product listings). Without this, the toddler loop asks 'what is this?' 30 times for the same kind of element. The sieve should detect structural repetition (N children of a container with the same tag/class/nesting pattern), report it as a collection with a template and count, and the toddler loop should present the collection as one group for single-classification. Example: 'I found 30 items that look like [link + domain + score]. What are these?' → Human says 'StoryList.story' → all 30 classified at once. Demo app may include collections depending on how fast we go.

title: Demo app — standalone login page for demo acts
description: Standalone HTML page served locally. Login form with email, password, submit, forgot password link. Known element inventory (~7-10 elements). Supports the add Remember Me checkbox code change (3-line diff). Must be runnable by sl run with Gherkin tests.
close_reason: Standalone Express demo app with login page, dashboard, session handling, 10 sieveable elements

title: diff tool — structural delta between observations
description: Compute and present structural differences between two sieve outputs. What elements appeared, disappeared, changed state.
notes: Promoted to demo scope. TL UI implementation is leftglove-cuo. This issue can be closed in favor of that one, or kept if we also want a standalone diff tool/MCP tool later.
close_reason: Absorbed into leftglove-cuo (TL UI sieve diff display). If standalone diff MCP tool needed later, create new bead.

title: Glossary generation — toddler labels to SL glossary EDN
description: Classification JSON from toddler UI to SL-compatible glossary EDN files. Could be script, TL UI feature, or MCP server piece. For demo may hand-write glossary, but pipeline should exist.
close_reason: Absorbed into leftglove-07k (TL UI Write Glossary integration). SL glossary HTTP endpoints (leftglove-cms) already built — the real task is the TL UI button that calls them.

title: Verify sl run + strict mode against demo app
description: Confirm sl run executes Gherkin against demo app and passes. Strict mode rejects unknown glossary refs. After deleting Remember Me checkbox, sl run fails clearly. SL work tracked here because it blocks demo.
close_reason: Premature — demo app still being built in LG. Will open appropriate verification bead once demo stabilizes.
